name: MATHS
description: Analog function generator. Four channels: two full-featured (1 & 4) with rise/fall/curve and cycle, two attenuverter/offset channels (2 & 3) with unity mixing.

controls.name: Curve
controls.detail: CCW: expo · noon: linear · CW: log

controls.name: Attenuverters
controls.detail: CCW: invert · noon: zero · CW: unity

controls.name: Offset
controls.description: DC offset. Adds to SUM output.

inputs.name: CH 1 Rise CV
inputs.description: Voltage control of CH 1 rise time

inputs.name: CH 1 Fall CV
inputs.description: Voltage control of CH 1 fall time

inputs.name: CH 4 Rise CV
inputs.description: Voltage control of CH 4 rise time

inputs.name: CH 4 Fall CV
inputs.description: Voltage control of CH 4 fall time

outputs.name: CH 1 EOR
outputs.description: End-of-rise gate from CH 1 only. Goes high at peak of CH 1 envelope.

outputs.name: CH 4 EOC
outputs.description: End-of-cycle gate from CH 4 only. Goes high at end of CH 4 fall stage.

outputs.name: Unity
outputs.description: Sum of all 4 channel outputs at unity gain

outputs.name: OR
outputs.description: Maximum (analog OR) of CH 1 and CH 4

behaviors.name: Cycle Mode
behaviors.description: Self-triggering LFO. Speed set by Rise + Fall. LED indicates cycle. Syncs to trigger input when patched — rising edge resets the cycle, enabling clock-locked LFOs.

behaviors.name: Envelope
behaviors.description: Patch gate to TRIG for ASR (sustain during gate high, release on gate low). Patch gate to SIGNAL IN for the same behavior with the signal path active. Patch trigger for AD — rise fires on trigger, fall fires immediately at peak.

behaviors.name: Slew Limiter
behaviors.description: Portamento / glide. Patch pitch CV to SIGNAL IN with nothing in TRIG. Rise = glide up speed, Fall = glide down. Set Rise CCW and Fall CW for instant-up, slow-down portamento.

behaviors.name: Retrigger blocking
behaviors.description: MATHS ignores triggers arriving during Rise. Only retriggers during Fall. Enables analog clock division — set Rise time longer than the incoming clock period to skip every other trigger.

behaviors.name: Vari-Response curve
behaviors.description: Full CCW = logarithmic curve (slow start, fast finish — sounds natural on decay). Noon = linear. Full CW = exponential (fast start, slow tail — snappy attack feel). Applies to both Rise and Fall simultaneously; use CH2/3 attenuverters to offset the resulting shape asymmetrically.

behaviors.name: SUM / OR bus normalization
behaviors.description: Patching a cable from CH 1 OUT or CH 4 OUT removes that channel from the SUM and OR buses. You cannot use an individual output AND have it contribute to SUM/OR simultaneously — use a mult or stackable if you need both.

behaviors.name: CH 2 / CH 3 normalization gotcha
behaviors.trigger: CH 2 or CH 3 attenuverter at non-noon position with nothing patched
behaviors.description: CH 2 Signal In normalizes to an internal ±10V reference; CH 3 normalizes to +5V DC. Attenuverters at non-noon with nothing patched inject DC offset into the SUM output. Set both to noon or insert a dummy cable to eliminate bleed.

behaviors.name: Analog OR for envelope priority
behaviors.description: OR OUT always follows whichever of CH 1 or CH 4 is higher at any moment. Two envelopes with different timing on OR OUT produces a combined shape that always takes the longest/loudest — useful for VCA accent without mixing.

behaviors.name: EOC / EOR as clock outputs
behaviors.description: CH 4 EOC fires a gate at the end of every fall — use as clock output or to trigger other modules. CH 1 EOR fires at the peak of its rise. Chaining EOR → CH 4 TRIG and EOC → CH 1 TRIG creates quadrature LFOs (90° phase offset) without Cycle mode.

behaviors.name: BOTH input normalization
behaviors.trigger: patching to Trigger or Signal In
behaviors.description: The TRIG and SIGNAL IN jacks are normalled — a signal patched to TRIG also acts on SIGNAL IN unless SIGNAL IN is separately patched. This means a gate patched to TRIG controls both timing and signal path simultaneously by default.

patchIdeas.name: Bouncing ball (self-accelerating envelope)
patchIdeas.patch: CH 1 Cycle ON · CH 1 OUT → CH 1 Fall CV (attenuate via CH 2 attenuverter at ~2 o'clock before patching to Fall CV) · positive feedback shortens fall each cycle · envelope speeds up like a bouncing ball losing energy · adjust CH 2 attenuverter to set acceleration rate
patchIdeas.diagram:
MATHS:
* CH 1: Cycle ON
* CH 2: Attenuverter ~2 o'clock

- MATHS.CH 1 (OUT) >> MATHS.CH 2 (In)
- MATHS.CH 2 (Out) >> MATHS.CH 1 (Fall CV) // shortens fall each cycle

patchIdeas.name: Quadrature LFOs
patchIdeas.patch: CH 1 EOR → CH 4 TRIG · CH 4 EOC → CH 1 TRIG · Cycle OFF on both · Rise and Fall matched on both channels · CH 1 OUT and CH 4 OUT = two LFOs 90° apart · patch to filter cutoff + VCA for animated timbral motion
patchIdeas.diagram:
MATHS:
* CH 1: Cycle OFF, Rise + Fall matched
* CH 4: Cycle OFF, Rise + Fall matched

- MATHS.CH 1 (EOR) g> MATHS.CH 4 (Trig)
- MATHS.CH 4 (EOC) g> MATHS.CH 1 (Trig)
- MATHS.CH 1 (OUT) >> Filter (Cutoff)
- MATHS.CH 4 (OUT) >> VCA (CV)

patchIdeas.name: ASR envelope with sustain plateau
patchIdeas.patch: Gate → CH 1 SIGNAL IN (not TRIG) · Rise = attack time · Fall = release time · while gate is high, output holds at full voltage as a sustain plateau · Gate → TRIG gives AD only; Signal In gives true ASR
patchIdeas.diagram:
MATHS:
* CH 1: Rise = attack, Fall = release

- Gate Source (Gate) g> MATHS.CH 1 (Signal In) // not TRIG — gives true ASR
- MATHS.CH 1 (OUT) >> VCA (CV)

patchIdeas.name: Push-pull crossfade
patchIdeas.patch: CH 1 Cycle ON, slow rate · CH 1 OUT → VCA 1 CV (opens) · CH 1 INV (via CH 2 attenuverter full CW on CH 1 OUT) → VCA 2 CV (closes) · SUM = combined output · MATHS envelope simultaneously fades between two sound sources
patchIdeas.diagram:
MATHS:
* CH 1: Cycle ON, slow rate
* CH 2: Attenuverter full CCW (invert)

- MATHS.CH 1 (OUT) >> VCA 1 (CV) // opens
- MATHS.CH 1 (OUT) >> MATHS.CH 2 (In)
- MATHS.CH 2 (Out) >> VCA 2 (CV) // inverted — closes
- VCA 1 (Out) -> Mixer (In)
- VCA 2 (Out) -> Mixer (In)

patchIdeas.name: Envelope follower
patchIdeas.patch: Audio source → CH 4 SIGNAL IN · CH 4 Rise at noon · CH 4 Fall adjusted CW for slower tracking or CCW for tighter tracking · OR OUT = positive-only envelope following audio amplitude · use OR OUT to modulate filter cutoff for auto-wah
patchIdeas.diagram:
MATHS:
* CH 4: Rise noon, Fall CW for slower tracking

- Audio Source (Out) -> MATHS.CH 4 (Signal In)
- MATHS (OR) >> Filter (Cutoff) // auto-wah envelope

patchIdeas.name: Complex modulation shape (dual LFO mixer)
patchIdeas.patch: CH 1 Cycle ON · Rise + Fall set for slow rate (4–8s total) · CH 4 Cycle ON · Rise + Fall set for fast rate (0.5–1s total) · CH 2 attenuverter at 1 o'clock · CH 3 attenuverter at 11 o'clock · SUM OUT = slow + fast LFO combined with offset asymmetry · patch to filter FM
patchIdeas.diagram:
MATHS:
* CH 1: Cycle ON, slow (4-8s)
* CH 4: Cycle ON, fast (0.5-1s)
* CH 2: Attenuverter ~1 o'clock
* CH 3: Attenuverter ~11 o'clock

- MATHS.CH 1 (OUT) >> MATHS.CH 2 (In)
- MATHS.CH 4 (OUT) >> MATHS.CH 3 (In)
- MATHS (Unity) >> Filter (FM) // combined dual-LFO shape

patchIdeas.name: Analog clock divider
patchIdeas.patch: Fast clock → CH 1 TRIG · CH 1 Rise set longer than clock period · retrigger blocked during Rise phase · CH 1 EOR = every other trigger · CH 1 OUT → CH 4 TRIG for further division · stack EOR/EOC for /2, /4 outputs from one clock
patchIdeas.diagram:
MATHS:
* CH 1: Rise longer than clock period
* CH 4: Rise longer than CH 1 OUT period

- Clock Source (Clk) c> MATHS.CH 1 (Trig)
- MATHS.CH 1 (EOR) g> Destination (/2 Clock)
- MATHS.CH 1 (OUT) g> MATHS.CH 4 (Trig)
- MATHS.CH 4 (EOC) g> Destination (/4 Clock)

patchIdeas.name: Directional portamento (asymmetric glide)
patchIdeas.patch: Pitch CV → CH 1 SIGNAL IN · CH 1 Rise CCW (fast up) · CH 1 Fall 2 o'clock (slow down) · Curve noon · CH 1 OUT → VCO V/Oct · pitch snaps up instantly, glides down slowly · reverse Rise/Fall for opposite feel
patchIdeas.diagram:
MATHS:
* CH 1: Rise CCW (fast up), Fall ~2 o'clock (slow down), Curve noon

- Sequencer (Pitch) p> MATHS.CH 1 (Signal In)
- MATHS.CH 1 (OUT) p> VCO (V/Oct) // snaps up, glides down

patchIdeas.name: Sub-harmonic generator
patchIdeas.patch: Audio-rate VCO OUT → CH 4 TRIG · CH 4 Cycle mode ON · CH 4 Rise and Fall short but longer than one VCO cycle · CH 4 EOC = sub-octave pulse output · mix CH 4 EOC with VCO audio at SUM for Trautonium-style bass reinforcement
patchIdeas.diagram:
MATHS:
* CH 4: Cycle ON, Rise + Fall short (> 1 VCO cycle)

- VCO (Out) -> MATHS.CH 4 (Trig) // audio-rate trigger
- MATHS.CH 4 (EOC) -> Mixer (In) // sub-octave pulse

patchIdeas.name: Voltage comparator / gate extractor
patchIdeas.patch: Slow CV or audio → CH 2 SIGNAL IN · CH 2 attenuverter full CW · CH 2 OUT → CH 1 SIGNAL IN · CH 1 Rise and Fall both full CCW · CH 1 EOR outputs a gate whenever the input signal exceeds the CH 1 threshold · use EOR to trigger other events from a crossing point
patchIdeas.diagram:
MATHS:
* CH 2: Attenuverter full CW
* CH 1: Rise + Fall full CCW (instant)

- CV Source (Out) >> MATHS.CH 2 (In)
- MATHS.CH 2 (Out) >> MATHS.CH 1 (Signal In)
- MATHS.CH 1 (EOR) g> Destination (Trig) // gate on threshold crossing

patchIdeas.name: Coupled oscillators (quadrature-triggered pair)
patchIdeas.patch: CH 1 EOR → CH 4 TRIG · CH 4 EOC → CH 1 TRIG · Cycle OFF on both · briefly engage CH 1 Cycle to kick the pair off, then release · channels ping-pong and free-run 90° apart without Cycle mode engaged · self-sustaining quadrature without external clock
patchIdeas.diagram:
MATHS:
* CH 1: Cycle OFF (briefly ON to kick off)
* CH 4: Cycle OFF

- MATHS.CH 1 (EOR) g> MATHS.CH 4 (Trig)
- MATHS.CH 4 (EOC) g> MATHS.CH 1 (Trig) // self-sustaining pair
- MATHS.CH 1 (OUT) >> Destination A (CV)
- MATHS.CH 4 (OUT) >> Destination B (CV)

patchIdeas.name: Formant complex oscillator
patchIdeas.patch: CH 1 Cycle ON at audio rate · Rise very short · Curve full CW (expo) for sharp formant pulse · CH 4 Cycle ON at slow LFO rate · CH 4 OUT → CH 1 Rise CV · modulating Rise sweeps formant peak like a Mangrove-style complex oscillator
patchIdeas.diagram:
MATHS:
* CH 1: Cycle ON, audio rate, Rise short, Curve full CW
* CH 4: Cycle ON, slow LFO

- MATHS.CH 4 (OUT) >> MATHS.CH 1 (Rise CV) // sweeps formant
- MATHS.CH 1 (OUT) -> VCA (In)

patchIdeas.name: Self-modulating cycle (audio-rate FM)
patchIdeas.patch: CH 1 Cycle ON at audio rate · CH 1 OUT → CH 2 SIGNAL IN · CH 2 attenuverter moderate · CH 2 contributes to SUM · SUM OUT → CH 1 Rise CV · signal FMs its own rise time producing chaotic pitched FM · tame via CH 2 attenuverter
patchIdeas.diagram:
MATHS:
* CH 1: Cycle ON, audio rate
* CH 2: Attenuverter ~1-2 o'clock

- MATHS.CH 1 (OUT) >> MATHS.CH 2 (In)
- MATHS (Unity) >> MATHS.CH 1 (Rise CV) // self-FM feedback
- MATHS.CH 1 (OUT) -> VCA (In)

patchIdeas.name: Krell patch (self-evolving random)
patchIdeas.patch: CH 4 Cycle ON · CH 4 EOC → S&H TRIG · Noise → S&H IN · S&H OUT → CH 4 Rise CV · each cycle samples new random voltage that sets the next rise time · envelope durations wander autonomously · classic Todd Barton Krell from one MATHS channel
patchIdeas.diagram:
MATHS:
* CH 4: Cycle ON

- MATHS.CH 4 (EOC) t> S&H (Trig)
- Noise (Out) >> S&H (In)
- S&H (Out) >> MATHS.CH 4 (Rise CV) // random rise time each cycle
- MATHS.CH 4 (OUT) >> VCA (CV)

patchIdeas.name: Trigger-to-gate converter (VC pulse width)
patchIdeas.patch: Short trigger → CH 1 TRIG · CH 1 Rise sets trigger delay · CH 1 Fall sets output gate width · CH 1 EOR goes high at end of Rise and stays high through Fall · use Rise CV for voltage-controlled delay, Fall CV for voltage-controlled pulse width
patchIdeas.diagram:
MATHS:
* CH 1: Rise = delay, Fall = gate width

- Trigger Source (Trig) t> MATHS.CH 1 (Trig)
- MATHS.CH 1 (EOR) g> Destination (Gate) // delayed gate of Fall width

patchIdeas.name: Bipolar VCA (attenuverter trick)
patchIdeas.patch: Signal → CH 2 SIGNAL IN · CH 2 attenuverter = manual bipolar level · CH 2 OUT = scaled/inverted copy of input · no envelope required — MATHS used purely as manual-control bipolar VCA / inverter · identical trick on CH 3 with different normalization
patchIdeas.diagram:
MATHS:
* CH 2: Attenuverter = level (CCW invert, CW unity)

- Signal Source (Out) -> MATHS.CH 2 (In)
- MATHS.CH 2 (Out) -> Destination (In) // bipolar scaled/inverted copy

patchIdeas.name: Voltage-controlled pulse delay
patchIdeas.patch: Clock → CH 1 TRIG · CV source → CH 1 Rise CV · each incoming trigger is delayed by the CV-controlled Rise time before CH 1 EOR fires · use to shift a clock lane relative to master for swing / phase offsets on demand
patchIdeas.diagram:
MATHS:
* CH 1: Rise CV = delay amount

- Clock Source (Clk) c> MATHS.CH 1 (Trig)
- CV Source (Out) >> MATHS.CH 1 (Rise CV)
- MATHS.CH 1 (EOR) g> Destination (Clk) // delayed by CV

patchIdeas.name: Full-wave rectifier (absolute value)
patchIdeas.patch: Bipolar signal → CH 1 SIGNAL IN (passes positive half) · same signal → CH 4 SIGNAL IN after inversion via CH 2 full CCW (passes negative half, flipped) · OR OUT takes max of CH 1 and CH 4 = absolute value · use for envelope-follower front end or unusual wavefolding
patchIdeas.diagram:
MATHS:
* CH 2: Attenuverter full CCW (invert)
* CH 1 & CH 4: Rise + Fall full CCW (pass-through)

- Signal Source (Out) -> MATHS.CH 1 (Signal In)
- Signal Source (Out) -> MATHS.CH 2 (In)
- MATHS.CH 2 (Out) -> MATHS.CH 4 (Signal In) // inverted copy
- MATHS (OR) -> Destination (In) // |signal|

patchIdeas.name: Pseudo-VCA with soft clip
patchIdeas.patch: Audio → CH 1 SIGNAL IN · CV → CH 2 SIGNAL IN · CH 2 attenuverter sets modulation depth · SUM OUT combines audio with CV offset = AM-scaled output that saturates at SUM rails · no true multiplication but usable as a gritty VCA substitute
patchIdeas.diagram:
MATHS:
* CH 1: Rise + Fall CCW (pass-through)
* CH 2: Attenuverter sets AM depth

- Audio Source (Out) -> MATHS.CH 1 (Signal In)
- CV Source (Out) >> MATHS.CH 2 (In)
- MATHS (Unity) -> Destination (In) // AM-scaled with soft clip

patchIdeas.name: Stepped random modulation (no S&H module)
patchIdeas.patch: Clock → CH 1 TRIG · CH 1 EOR → CH 4 TRIG · Noise or slow LFO → CH 4 SIGNAL IN · CH 4 Rise + Fall full CCW so the channel tracks input at trigger moments only · CH 4 OUT = stepped random modulation derived purely from MATHS + noise
patchIdeas.diagram:
MATHS:
* CH 1: Rise short, Fall short
* CH 4: Rise + Fall full CCW (sample-on-trig)

- Clock Source (Clk) c> MATHS.CH 1 (Trig)
- MATHS.CH 1 (EOR) g> MATHS.CH 4 (Trig)
- Noise (Out) >> MATHS.CH 4 (Signal In)
- MATHS.CH 4 (OUT) >> Destination (CV) // stepped random

patchIdeas.name: Triple cross-modulated LFO ring
patchIdeas.patch: CH 1 and CH 4 Cycle ON at different rates · CH 1 OUT → CH 2 IN (attenuated) · CH 4 OUT → CH 3 IN (attenuated) · SUM OUT → CH 1 Rise CV via mult · CH 4 OUT → CH 1 Fall CV · three-node cross-coupled modulation ring produces ever-shifting complex LFO
patchIdeas.diagram:
MATHS:
* CH 1: Cycle ON
* CH 4: Cycle ON (different rate)
* CH 2 & CH 3: Attenuverters moderate

- MATHS.CH 1 (OUT) >> MATHS.CH 2 (In)
- MATHS.CH 4 (OUT) >> MATHS.CH 3 (In)
- MATHS (Unity) >> MATHS.CH 1 (Rise CV) // cross-mod
- MATHS.CH 4 (OUT) >> MATHS.CH 1 (Fall CV)

patchIdeas.name: Ratchet / burst generator
patchIdeas.patch: Gate → CH 1 TRIG · CH 1 set for short AD envelope · CH 1 OUT → CH 4 Rise CV · CH 4 Cycle ON · CH 4 only cycles while CH 1 envelope is high, accelerating as envelope rises · CH 4 EOC → drum VCA envelope trigger · yields a ratcheted burst per incoming gate
patchIdeas.diagram:
MATHS:
* CH 1: Rise short, Fall short (AD)
* CH 4: Cycle ON

- Gate Source (Gate) g> MATHS.CH 1 (Trig)
- MATHS.CH 1 (OUT) >> MATHS.CH 4 (Rise CV) // windows + accelerates CH 4
- MATHS.CH 4 (EOC) t> VCA Env (Trig) // ratchet bursts

patchIdeas.name: Classic Krell with dual random CVs
patchIdeas.patch: Todd Barton Krell pattern expanded across MATHS plus two external S&H channels. CH 4 Cycle ON · CH 4 EOR → S&H 1 trig + S&H 2 trig · Noise → both S&H inputs · S&H 1 → CH 4 Rise CV (random attack each cycle) · S&H 2 → CH 4 Fall CV (random decay) · CH 4 OUT through quantizer → VCO V/Oct · CH 4 OUT → VCA CV · self-evolving melody with random attack, decay and pitch on every note.
patchIdeas.diagram:
MATHS:
* CH 4: Cycle ON

- MATHS.CH 4 (EOR) t> S&H 1 (Trig)
- MATHS.CH 4 (EOR) t> S&H 2 (Trig)
- Noise (Out) >> S&H 1 (In)
- Noise (Out) >> S&H 2 (In)
- S&H 1 (Out) >> MATHS.CH 4 (Rise CV) // random attack each cycle
- S&H 2 (Out) >> MATHS.CH 4 (Fall CV) // random decay each cycle
- MATHS.CH 4 (OUT) >> Quantizer (In)
- Quantizer (Out) p> VCO (V/Oct)
- MATHS.CH 4 (OUT) >> VCA (CV)
- VCO (Out) -> VCA (In) -> Output (In)

patchIdeas.name: Buchla low-pass-gate bongo
patchIdeas.patch: West-coast plucked resonance using MATHS as the gate envelope. Clock → CH 1 TRIG · CH 1 Rise very short, Fall medium, Curve CW (expo) · CH 1 OUT → VCA CV (acts as LPG contour) · random CV → VCO pitch · VCO → VCA audio in · short percussive pings follow the envelope's expo decay giving the classic DPO-plus-Optomix bongo character without a dedicated LPG.
patchIdeas.diagram:
MATHS:
* CH 1: Rise very short, Fall medium, Curve full CW (expo)

- Clock (Out) c> MATHS.CH 1 (Trig)
- Random CV (Out) p> VCO (V/Oct)
- VCO (Out) -> VCA (In)
- MATHS.CH 1 (OUT) >> VCA (CV) // LPG-style plucked contour
- VCA (Out) -> Output (In) // Buchla bongo

patchIdeas.name: Self-patched Both (CH 1 ↔ CH 4 cross-mod)
patchIdeas.patch: Classic MATHS illustrated 'Both' patch. CH 1 Cycle ON · CH 4 Cycle ON · CH 1 OUT → CH 4 Rise CV (via CH 3 attenuverter to tame) · CH 4 OUT → CH 1 Fall CV (via CH 2 attenuverter) · each channel reshapes the other's timing, producing warping complex LFO shapes · SUM OUT → VCO V/Oct for chaotic melodic drift.
patchIdeas.diagram:
MATHS:
* CH 1: Cycle ON
* CH 4: Cycle ON
* CH 2 & CH 3: Attenuverters moderate

- MATHS.CH 1 (OUT) >> MATHS.CH 3 (In)
- MATHS.CH 3 (Out) >> MATHS.CH 4 (Rise CV) // CH 1 warps CH 4
- MATHS.CH 4 (OUT) >> MATHS.CH 2 (In)
- MATHS.CH 2 (Out) >> MATHS.CH 1 (Fall CV) // CH 4 warps CH 1
- MATHS (Unity) p> VCO (V/Oct) // summed chaotic pitch

patchIdeas.name: Slew + sum + inverter (three jobs, one module)
patchIdeas.patch: MATHS as a utility swiss-army knife. Sequencer CV → CH 1 SIGNAL IN, Rise short + Fall short = portamento slew → VCO pitch · LFO → CH 4 SIGNAL IN with CH 4 used as inverter via SUM polarity (or mult through CH 2 attenuverter full CCW) → filter cutoff · CH 2 + CH 3 summed = mixed modulation → VCA CV · one MATHS simultaneously provides glide, inversion and CV summing for a whole voice.
patchIdeas.diagram:
MATHS:
* CH 1: Rise short, Fall short (slew)
* CH 2: Attenuverter full CCW (invert)
* CH 3: Attenuverter ~1 o'clock

- Sequencer (Pitch) p> MATHS.CH 1 (Signal In)
- MATHS.CH 1 (OUT) p> VCO (V/Oct) // slewed pitch
- LFO (Out) >> MATHS.CH 2 (In)
- MATHS.CH 2 (Out) >> Filter (Cutoff) // inverted LFO
- Mod A (Out) >> MATHS.CH 3 (In)
- MATHS (Unity) >> VCA (CV) // CH 2+CH 3 summed
